---
title: "Scenario: Team Collaboration & Data Handoff"
description: >
  Share reproducible analysis states with collaborators and across environments
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

When multiple researchers work on the same dataset, or when analyses need to be handed off for review, data provenance becomes critical. OmicsLake provides versioned snapshots and lineage tracking to ensure everyone works from the same, documented data states.

## Snapshot Naming for Teams

Establish clear naming conventions:

```r
# Recommended: date + analyst + description
lake$snap("2025-03-18_yuki_qc_complete")
lake$snap("2025-03-20_tanaka_normalization_v2")
lake$snap("2025-03-25_submission_final")

# Record context with each snapshot
ol_commit(
  note = "QC filtering complete. Removed 2,341 low-quality cells.",
  params = list(
    analyst = "Yuki Matsui",
    min_genes = 500,
    max_mito_pct = 10
  )
)
```

## Sharing a Lake

### Option A: Share the directory (simplest)

```r
# The entire lake is in ~/.omicslake/<project>/
# Package and share
tar("rnaseq_project.tar.gz", "~/.omicslake/rnaseq_project", compression = "gzip")
```

The recipient unpacks it to their `~/.omicslake/` and opens it:

```r
lake <- Lake$new("rnaseq_project")
lake$history()  # See all snapshots
lake$tree("de_results")  # Inspect lineage
```

### Option B: Export specific datasets as Parquet

For collaborators who use Python or only need specific results:

```r
lake$export("de_results", "shared/de_results.parquet")
lake$export("normalized_counts", "shared/normalized_counts.parquet")
lake$export("sample_info", "shared/sample_info.parquet")
```

### Option C: Git-based synchronization

For ongoing collaboration, include the lake in version control:

```r
# Add lake to .gitignore (DuckDB files are binary)
# Instead, export snapshots to Parquet in a tracked directory
dir.create("data/snapshots", recursive = TRUE)
for (tbl in lake$tables()) {
  lake$export(tbl, file.path("data/snapshots", paste0(tbl, ".parquet")))
}
# Git track the Parquet files (or use Git LFS for large ones)
```

## Reproducing a Collaborator's Analysis

When you receive a lake from a colleague:

```r
# 1. Open the lake
lake <- Lake$new("received_project")

# 2. Check available snapshots
lake$history()
# label                          created_at
# 2025-03-18_yuki_qc_complete    2025-03-18 14:30:00
# 2025-03-20_yuki_normalized     2025-03-20 09:15:00
# 2025-03-22_yuki_de_complete    2025-03-22 16:45:00

# 3. Restore to their final state
lake$restore("2025-03-22_yuki_de_complete")

# 4. Inspect lineage
lake$tree("de_results")

# 5. Verify the data
de <- lake$get("de_results")
dim(de)
head(de)

# 6. Build on their work
lake$ref("de_results") |>
  dplyr::filter(padj < 0.01, abs(log2FoldChange) > 2) |>
  save_as("stringent_hits", lake)

lake$snap("2025-03-25_tanaka_stringent_filter")
```

## Parallel Analysis Branches

Multiple analysts working from the same raw data:

```r
# Analyst A: DESeq2 approach
lake$restore("qc_complete")
# ... DESeq2 analysis ...
lake$snap("analyst_a_deseq2")

# Analyst B: edgeR approach
lake$restore("qc_complete")
# ... edgeR analysis ...
lake$snap("analyst_b_edger")

# Compare results
deseq_results <- lake$get("de_results_deseq2", "analyst_a_deseq2")
edger_results <- lake$get("de_results_edger", "analyst_b_edger")

# How many significant genes overlap?
deseq_sig <- deseq_results$gene_id[deseq_results$padj < 0.05]
edger_sig <- edger_results$gene_id[edger_results$padj < 0.05]
length(intersect(deseq_sig, edger_sig))
```

## The Complete Reproducibility Package

For maximum reproducibility, bundle four components:

```r
# 1. Lake directory (data + metadata + lineage)
tar("reproducibility_package/lake.tar.gz",
    "~/.omicslake/my_project", compression = "gzip")

# 2. renv lockfile (package versions)
renv::snapshot()
file.copy("renv.lock", "reproducibility_package/renv.lock")

# 3. Analysis scripts (Git)
# git archive HEAD -o reproducibility_package/code.tar.gz

# 4. Session info
writeLines(capture.output(sessionInfo()),
           "reproducibility_package/session_info.txt")
```

### Recipient's workflow

```r
# 1. Restore package environment
renv::restore("renv.lock")

# 2. Unpack lake
untar("lake.tar.gz", exdir = "~/.omicslake/")

# 3. Open and verify
lake <- Lake$new("my_project")
lake$restore("submission_final")

# 4. Run analysis scripts
source("analysis.R")

# 5. Compare results
all.equal(
  lake$get("de_results"),
  read.csv("expected_results.csv")
)
```

## Handoff Checklist

Before sharing an analysis:

- [ ] Create a final snapshot with a descriptive label
- [ ] Verify all lineage edges are present (`tree()` for key outputs)
- [ ] Record analysis parameters with `ol_commit()`
- [ ] Export key results as Parquet for cross-platform access
- [ ] Include `renv.lock` for package reproducibility
- [ ] Document snapshot inventory and purpose of each
- [ ] Test restore on a clean environment if possible
